name: bear crawl
instructions: Start on all fours with your hands directly under your shoulders and your knees directly under your hips.
Lift your knees slightly off the ground, keeping your back flat and your core engaged.
Move your right hand and left foot forward simultaneously, followed by your left hand and right foot.
Continue crawling forward, alternating your hand and foot movements.
Maintain a steady pace and keep your core tight throughout the exercise.
Continue for the desired distance or time.

Start on all fours with your hands directly under your shoulders and your knees directly under your hips. Lift your knees slightly off the ground, keeping your back flat and your core engaged. Move your right hand and left foot forward simultaneously, followed by your left hand and right foot. Continue crawling forward, alternating your hand and foot movements. Maintain a steady pace and keep your core tight throughout the exercise. Continue for the desired distance or time.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
